meta_description: DeepOlea is a technology company that transforms artificial intelligence and machine learning into real-world IT solutions. Based in Gaziantep.
hero_sub: We transform artificial intelligence and machine learning into real-world IT solutions — with solid engineering, measured design, and lasting value.
card_1_desc: Data-driven models, forecasting, and deep learning. We bring research-level methods to reliable, scalable products.
card_2_desc: Web, mobile, and cloud-based applications. Solid architecture, clean code, and sustainable systems.
card_3_desc: AI-supported platforms for accreditation, evaluation, and process management; tools that make institutional work easier.
section_story_p1: Olea is the Latin name for the olive tree — the ancient Mediterranean symbol of wisdom, the gift of Athena. Deep-rooted, patient, enduring.
section_story_p2: Deep is the language of deep learning; of artificial intelligence that thinks in layers and sees patterns.
section_story_p3: DeepOlea unites these two: technology that thinks deeply, grounded in deep-rooted wisdom.
section_contact_desc: Do you have an idea, a project, or a question? We'd be happy to talk.
projects_page_desc: Deep learning models under development, research conducted with U-Net and Autoencoder architectures, and other software projects will be listed here soon.
proj_card_fisformer_desc: An innovative architecture that replaces the conventional self-attention mechanism in Transformers with a Fuzzy Inference System (FIS) to model uncertainty and improve interpretability.
proj_fisformer_title: FISformer: Replacing Self-Attention with a Fuzzy Inference System in Transformer Models for Time Series Forecasting
proj_fisformer_abstract: Transformers have achieved remarkable progress in time series forecasting, yet their reliance on deterministic dot-product attention limits their capacity to model uncertainty and nonlinear dependencies across multivariate temporal dimensions. To address this limitation, we propose FISformer, a Fuzzy Inference System-Driven Transformer that replaces conventional attention with a FIS Interaction mechanism. In this framework, each query-key pair undergoes a fuzzy inference process for every feature dimension, where learnable membership functions and rule-based reasoning estimate token-wise relational strengths. These FIS-derived interaction weights capture uncertainty and provide interpretable, continuous mappings between tokens. A softmax operation is applied along the token axis to normalize these weights, which are then combined with the corresponding value features through element-wise multiplication to yield the final context-enhanced token representations. This design fuses the interpretability and uncertainty modeling of fuzzy logic with the representational power of Transformers. Extensive experiments on multiple benchmark datasets demonstrate that FISformer achieves superior forecasting accuracy, noise robustness, and interpretability compared to state-of-the-art Transformer variants, establishing fuzzy inference as an effective alternative to conventional attention mechanisms.
proj_fisformer_method_desc: Instead of conventional dot-product similarity, FISformer embeds a complete first-order Sugeno Fuzzy Inference System into the query-key interaction. This operates in four key stages:
proj_fisformer_method_1: Fuzzification: Query and key embeddings are mapped to fuzzy membership degrees using learnable Gaussian membership functions.
proj_fisformer_method_2: Rule Generation: Fuzzy rules are formulated using a product-based t-norm to compute the firing strength of each rule.
proj_fisformer_method_3: Defuzzification: The activations of all rules are normalized and aggregated to produce a defuzzified Fuzzy Interaction Map.
proj_fisformer_method_4: FIS Interaction: A softmax operation normalizes these weights, which are then combined with value features through element-wise multiplication.
proj_fisformer_dataset_desc: The proposed model's robustness and generality were evaluated on 7 real-world multivariate time series datasets spanning energy, meteorology, transportation, and finance:
proj_tab1_desc: TABLE I. Detailed dataset descriptions including input dimensions, number of samples, prediction sequence length, and time frequency.
proj_fisformer_perf_desc: FISformer consistently outperformed state-of-the-art architectures like Informer, Autoformer, Fedformer, and iTransformer in MSE and MAE metrics. Furthermore, ablation studies proved the superiority of the FIS mechanism over standard Self-Attention.
proj_tab2_desc: Table II. Average forecasting performance in terms of MSE and MAE across different prediction lengths. The best results are highlighted in red, and the second-best results are underlined blue.
fig1_caption: Fig. 1. Overall architecture of the proposed FISformer model.
fig2_caption: Fig. 2. FIS-based Token Interaction via learnable Gaussian membership functions.
